description_long: This is a rustic, wooden bucket or tankard with a cylindrical body constructed from vertically aligned wooden staves. The exterior is reinforced by three prominent, metallic-looking bands, each with visible metal fasteners, giving it a sturdy, medieval appearance. The top rim is uneven and jagged, suggesting wear or intentional design, and the base is thick and slightly flared for stability. A robust, wooden handle is attached to one side, secured by matching bands, making it easy to grip and carry. The interior appears hollow, suitable for holding liquids or small items, and the overall finish is matte with visible wood grain and texture. The coloration is predominantly dark brown with lighter, coppery metallic accents on the bands and handle attachments.
description: A sturdy, dark wooden bucket or tankard with metallic bands and a thick handle, featuring a jagged rim and a robust, rustic design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the thick wooden staves, two metal bands, and the handle attachment facing forward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full handle and the attachment points of the metal bands, with the jagged rim visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the uninterrupted cylindrical wooden body and all three metal bands, with no handle visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle from a different angle and the full profile of the bucket's body.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops around body and base
